entry.title: Unlocking Success with SAP: Syed's Inspiring Journey to DemoJam Triumph
entry.content:
Syed couldn't imagine what it would feel like to be on a big stage. He couldn’t image the wild clapping and shouting coming from a crowded room of tech enthusiasts. He couldn't imagine the feeling he would have in his stomach when the six important minutes began – his team’s six important minutes. “I just thought, how can I be so lucky?” Syed was thrilled. He was driven by the excitement of the room – and his own. 

Syed used the SAP Learning site to quickly gain new skills.“It was my first time in a competition”, the 30-year-old from Bangalore recounts. With his colleague Navin, Syed presented their innovative solution addressing a supply chain problem in India: the issue of counterfeit drugs. And they did it on stage the SAP TechEd DemoJam in 2023. Six teams were given six minutes each to present their solution to a problem – live in front of hundreds of people. No mock-ups were allowed, their demo had to be flawless. “My mentor Chandramohan Sankaran, alongside my colleagues such as Kesavan and Vipin, actively contributed to my DemoJam journey. Their diligent efforts and valuable feedback significantly elevated our demo story, pushing it to new heights.” And they secured the first place in the competition.  

Newfound knowledge helped clinch the victory 

However, it didn’t come along that easy, the Senior Product Specialist at SAP Labs Bangalore explains. With only 15 to 20 days to prepare the demo, the duo turned to the SAP Learning site to quickly gain expertise in technologies such as SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Mobile Start, SAP S/4HANA Extension, SAP Adobe Forms, Integration and more. “The knowledge acquired played a pivotal role in delivering the best solution within the tight timeframe, ultimately leading to our success”, Syed says and adds: “I am grateful for the valuable resources provided by SAP, which significantly contributed to our achievements.” 

But Syed’s dedication to staying informed about the latest technologies and innovations does not only define his professional life as a Senior Product Specialist, where he strives to staying at the forefront of SAP advancements and contributing to the development and success of SAP Business Technology Platform. It has also long permeated into his private life as well, where the father of a 3-year-old daughter feels equally excited by cheering when he's in the kitchen. “I enjoy exploring new cuisines and trying out different recipes in my kitchen”, he says, stating that he is a Biryani expert who knows his way around the famous South Indian rice dish. It’s again a testament to his love for continuous learning.  

Broader skillset and industry recognition 

“Learning something new has always brought about significant positive changes in my life”, Syed says. “And acquiring knowledge and skills, particularly through platforms like the SAP Learning site, has enhanced my professional competence. It fostered a mindset of continuous improvement and problem-solving, enabling me to address challenges creatively. It has broadened my skillset and positively influenced various aspects of my professional life, including networking opportunities and industry recognition. In essence, continuous learning has played a pivotal role in my overall growth and success in the dynamic field of SAP technologies.”  

And for those who are still thinking about whether to make their appearance on a big stage and showcase their newfound skills, Syed has a clear message: “If I can do it – you can do it too.” 

Start your own success story   

Did you feel inspired by Syed’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories!

entry.title: Installing SAPRouter on Linux: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
What is SAP Router ?

SAPRouter is a software component used to secure communication between SAP systems and the internet. Installing SAPRouter on Linux is a crucial step in ensuring secure communication for your SAP landscape. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the installation process.

Prerequisites:

- Linux server (e.g., CentOS, Ubuntu)

- Root access to the server

- SAPRouter software package downloaded from the SAP Support Portal

Step 1: Download SAPRouter:

Download the SAPRouter software package from the SAP Support Portal. Ensure that you download the correct version for your operating system.

Step 2: Extract the SAPRouter Package:

Transfer the downloaded SAPRouter package to your Linux server. Use the following command to extract the package:

tar -xvf saprouter_<version>_linux_x86_64.tar.gz

Step 3: Create a Directory for SAPRouter:

Create a directory to store the SAPRouter files. You can use the following command to create the directory:

mkdir /usr/sap/saprouter

Step 4: Copy SAPRouter Files:

Copy the extracted SAPRouter files to the newly created directory:

cp -R <path_to_extracted_files>/saprouter /usr/sap/saprouter

Step 5: Create a Configuration File:

Create a configuration file named `saprouter.ini` in the `/usr/sap/saprouter` directory. Here's a basic example of the configuration file:

# SAProuter Configuration File

version = 39

httpport = 81

tracefile = /usr/sap/saprouter/saprouter.trc

authid = *

permit = *

Step 6: Set Permissions:

Ensure that the SAPRouter binary and configuration files have the correct permissions:

chmod 755 /usr/sap/saprouter/saprouter

chmod 644 /usr/sap/saprouter/saprouter.ini

 

Step 7: Start SAPRouter:

Start the SAPRouter using the following command:

/usr/sap/saprouter/saprouter -r -R /usr/sap/saprouter/saprouter.ini

Step 8: Verify SAPRouter Status:

Verify that SAPRouter is running and listening on the specified port (e.g., 81):

netstat -tuln | grep 81

Step 9: Configure Firewall:

Configure your firewall to allow incoming and outgoing traffic on the SAPRouter port (e.g., 81) to ensure proper communication.

Step 10: Configure SAP Systems:

Update the `secinfo` file of your SAP systems to include the SAPRouter details for communication through the SAPRouter.

Overall information:

By following these steps, you can successfully install SAPRouter on your Linux server. This will help secure communication between your SAP systems and the internet, ensuring the integrity and confidentiality of your SAP landscape.

#SAP #SAPRouter #Linux #Installation SAP Young Thinkers #Red Hat Enterprise Linux SUSE Linux Enterprise Server SAP Women in Tech SAP Integration Suite SAP Business Application Studio @Muthumayandi_Yadava @Subramanian @Sap @YejinYun

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 11th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/07/11/sap-developer-news-july11th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP TechEd 2024

  • TechEd Event Website https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

SAP CodeJam Latin America Roadshow

  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2024 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇨🇱 🇦🇷 🇨🇴 (August 8th - 21st 2024): https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2024-latin-america-edition-august-8th-21st-2024/ba-p/13710015

July Developer Challenge on “Reverse APIs” - a reminder

  • July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs" blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/july-developer-challenge-quot-reverse-apis-quot/ba-p/13749653

openSAP Migration to learning.sap.com

  • New home of openSAP content: https://learning.sap.com/courses?page=1&subtype=mooc-content

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP TechEd 2024

2:18 SAP CodeJam Latin America Roadshow

2:52 July Developer Challenge on “Reverse APIs” - a reminder

3:51 openSAP Migration to learning.sap.com

TRANSCRIPT

[Tom] Hi there everybody, I'm here with Kasimir The Cat, the official SAP TechEd mascot and she's very excited for TechEd this year, as you can tell. We're doing lots to get ready for TechEd already, even though it's several months away. We've got some great new things planned for you this year. First of all, we want to bring more of the TechEd experience to you where you're at. So as already announced, we're going to have the TechEd On Tour events. These are going to be in-person events that focus on the hands-on exercise experience and we're going to bring them to more regions of the world and work together with the user groups to bring you that experience. So be on the lookout for more details about that soon.

But of course, we also have the TechEd Virtual experience, the free online event experience, And I know our team is working really hard on content for that already. We're going to be doing a Developer Keynote Deconstructed. So throughout the two days, we're going to bring you different parts of a developer keynote highlighting major new innovations for developers in all different parts of the SAP ecosystem.

And finally, we have Devtoberfest again this year, and we're going to be bringing you Devtoberfest wrapped around TechEd, a little different this year because we're going to do two weeks before the virtual TechEd and two weeks after the virtual TechEd with Devtoberfest. That way we can help you prepare for TechEd and also do deeper dive follow-ups to some of the key TechEd content through Devtoberfest. And as always, we'll have the big contest. It will also tie into exercises from the TechEd virtual event as well. So be able to look out over the next couple of weeks for information on all these great aspects of TechEd, and also look forward to seeing more of Casimir the cat. So say goodbye, everybody. Bye, Kasimir. [Meow!]

[Antonio] Hola, SAP Developers. We are less than a month away from the start of the SAP CodeJam Roadshow in Latin America. The CodeJams we will be doing are Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP Build. In São Paulo, Brazil, it's fully booked. But we still have available spaces in the events of Santiago, Chile, Buenos Aires, Argentina, Bogotá, Colombia. See you soon.

[DJ] Hello. We just wanted to quickly remind you that this month's SAP Developer Challenge on reverse APIs is well underway. What do we mean by reverse APIs? Well, basically, we get you to set up services using CAP and API endpoints in those services, and we call those endpoints automatically to check you set them up as required. There's tons to discover and learn about. By the time you see this SAP Developer News episode, we'll have released the next task, that'll be the fourth one, out of a total of 12 that you'll have to complete this month.

Next week, the tasks revolve around creating endpoints in a more plain RESTful style service. Anyway, the link to the main challenge blog post is in the description, so head on over to to the blog post now and get started with the tasks and don't forget to have fun!

[Tom] As someone who has been involved with openSAP since its very beginning, I'm happy to see that the content from openSAP lives on as a part of the SAP Learning website. Many of the courses have been migrated over to the learning site and you can now find them unified with all the other learning journeys and free content to help you prepare for certification. Now the old openSAP website is only going to be available till the end of July, so don't forget to go in and download any certifications or other completion documents that you have there. But most of the recent content is going to live on and I think we can look forward to seeing other openSAP style content in the future on the SAP Learning Platform. So be sure to check it out in its new home there.

entry.title: SAP ATTP overview, Architecture and features
entry.content:
SAP ATTP – Overview, Features and Architecture

SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) is a specialized solution designed for pharmaceutical companies to effectively manage serial numbers, minimize compliance costs, and strengthen supply chain integrity. Its robust features ensure comprehensive visibility and traceability of pharmaceuticals, fully compliant with global serialization regulations.

SAP ATTP Overview

  • Ensuring Visibility and Traceability: SAP ATTP's primary objective is to ensure seamless visibility and traceability of pharmaceuticals by meticulously adhering to stringent global regulations on serialization, tracking, tracing, and reporting. This commitment fosters transparency, accountability, and trustworthiness throughout the entire supply chain ecosystem, ultimately enhancing the integrity and reliability of pharmaceutical products.
  • Overseeing Complex Supply Chains: ATTP plays a pivotal role in managing intricate supply chains, ensuring product genuineness, and mitigating risks associated with counterfeit activities or illicit diversions. This enhances brand reputation, bolsters consumer trust, and ensures stringent regulatory compliance.
  • Centralizing data and analytics: By centralizing product data serialization, tracking, and incorporating comprehensive reporting and analytics capabilities, SAP ATTP empowers organizations to make informed, data-driven decisions, enabling continuous improvement and operational excellence in pharmaceutical supply chain management.
  • Strengthening Pharmaceutical Supply Chains: Through fostering transparency, accountability, and operational excellence, SAP ATTP strengthens pharmaceutical supply chains, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, reducing counterfeiting risks, and building enduring consumer trust in the authenticity and safety of pharmaceutical products.
  • Risk Mitigation: SAP ATTP employs advanced algorithms and data analytics to detect and mitigate risks associated with counterfeit products and unauthorized diversions within the supply chain. By implementing secure serialization processes and authentication mechanisms, it helps pharmaceutical companies protect their brands and maintain consumer trust in product authenticity.
  • Equipped with robust features and functionalities, SAP ATTP serves as a vital tool for pharmaceutical companies to streamline serialization processes, mitigate compliance risks, and enhance supply chain security, ultimately contributing to the advancement of public health and safety.

SAP ATTP Features

  • Serialization Management: Serialization management involves the systematic assignment and tracking of unique identifiers (e.g., serial numbers) to individual units of pharmaceutical products. It ensures compliance with global regulatory mandates by enabling precise identification and traceability throughout the supply chain.
  • Aggregation and Disaggregation: Aggregation refers to grouping serialized units into larger entities (e.g., bundles, cases) for streamlined handling and distribution. Disaggregation involves breaking down these larger entities into individual serialized units. These processes optimize inventory management and logistics while maintaining traceability and compliance with serialization requirements.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Regulatory compliance in pharmaceutical supply chains entails adhering to stringent serialization, tracking, and reporting regulations enforced by agencies such as FDA and EMA. SAP ATTP facilitates compliance by providing robust tools for data capture, audit trails, and regulatory reporting, ensuring adherence to mandated standards.
  • Product Traceability: Product traceability involves tracking the complete lifecycle of pharmaceutical products, from manufacturing to distribution and beyond. SAP ATTP enhances traceability by capturing and managing serialized data at every stage, enabling rapid identification and resolution of quality issues and facilitating efficient recall management.
  • Reporting: SAP ATTP offers advanced reporting capabilities for generating comprehensive insights into serialized product data, compliance status, and supply chain performance metrics. These reports support informed decision-making and regulatory submissions by providing actionable analytics on operational efficiencies and compliance metrics.
  • Integration: Integration capabilities within SAP ATTP enable seamless connectivity with ERP, MES, and logistics systems, ensuring real-time data synchronization and visibility across the supply chain. This integration facilitates efficient management of serialized product data and enhances collaboration among stakeholders in compliance and operational workflows.

Architecture of SAP – ATTP

The architecture is designed to integrate seamlessly into pharmaceutical manufacturing and distribution environments, providing end-to-end visibility and control over product serialization, traceability, and compliance. Each layer plays a critical role in ensuring data integrity, regulatory compliance, operational efficiency, and secure data management throughout the product lifecycle.

  • Database Layer: Responsible for managing structured data storage, including serialization data (unique identifiers for products), transactional information (events related to product movements), and detailed product specifications. It ensures robust scalability to handle large volumes of data and maintains data integrity through transactional consistency and relational database management.
  • Application Layer: This layer hosts the core business logic and application services of SAP ATTP. It executes complex algorithms for transaction processing, manages serialization processes (assigning, capturing, and verifying unique identifiers), handles aggregation (combining serialized units into larger containers), and enforces compliance with regulatory standards (such as track-and-trace requirements).
  • Integration Layer: Facilitates interoperability between SAP ATTP and other enterprise systems such as ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), WMS (Warehouse Management Systems), and external stakeholders' systems (regulatory authorities, trading partners). It supports various communication protocols (like web services, messaging queues) to ensure seamless data exchange, synchronization of serialization data, and real-time visibility across the supply chain network.
  • User Interface (UI) Layer: Provides multiple interfaces (web-based, desktop, and mobile) for users to interact with SAP ATTP. It offers functionalities for managing serialization activities (such as querying and updating product traceability data), monitoring compliance status, handling exceptions (like product recalls), and generating reports on serialization performance and regulatory adherence.
  • Security Layer: Implements robust security measures to safeguard sensitive serialization data and ensure regulatory compliance. This includes access control mechanisms (defining user roles and permissions), encryption of sensitive data during transmission and storage, authentication (verifying user identities), and authorization (granting access based on roles and permissions).
  • Analytics & Reporting Layer: Utilizes data analytics tools and reporting functionalities to generate actionable insights from serialization data. It provides predefined and customizable reports on supply chain performance, compliance metrics (like adherence to serialization regulations), and trends analysis. These insights help stakeholders make informed decisions, improve operational efficiencies, and ensure continuous compliance with regulatory requirements.

MAH View for SAP ATTP Architecture

The MAH (Marketing Authorization Holder) view in SAP ATTP serves as a specialized perspective within the system, focusing on enabling marketing authorization holders to manage serialization processes efficiently, ensure regulatory compliance, and gain actionable insights into supply chain operations.

MAH View encompasses several key functionalities and perspectives tailored to the needs of the marketing authorization holders in the pharmaceutical industry:

  • Authorization and Access: Customizes permissions and access levels specific to the roles of marketing authorization holders, ensuring appropriate visibility and control over serialization and traceability activities within the system.
  • Serialization Configuration: Empowers MAHs to configure serialization parameters such as serial number formats, aggregation rules, and other settings crucial for compliance with global regulatory standards. This capability enables MAHs to adapt to varying regulatory requirements across different markets efficiently.
  • Compliance Management: Supports MAHs in maintaining compliance with global serialization regulations through robust tools for serialization, traceability, and compliance reporting. This includes functionalities for capturing and managing serialization data, verifying product authenticity, and responding to regulatory queries or audits effectively.
  • Reporting and Analytics: Provides comprehensive reporting and analytics functionalities tailored to MAHs’ needs. This includes tracking serialization activities in real-time, generating compliance reports (such as serialization status reports and audit trails), and analysing supply chain performance to identify opportunities for optimization and regulatory adherence.

entry.title: Registration For SAP TechEd Virtual In 2024 Is Now Live!
entry.content:
It’s time to bring out your best again and join us for this year’s SAP TechEd Virtual! The high-energy, interactive and virtual experience is accessible to everyone and will take place on October 8-9, 2024. Spoiler: Registrants will benefit from an amazing goodie this year – so read on! 

Here’s what you can expect from SAP TechEd Virtual 

SAP TechEd is all about innovation and collaboration. By joining the virtual experience, you can gain the skills and expertise needed to advance your career and help your organization thrive using SAP technologies. Get ready to explore new tech, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. Plus: Hear firsthand customer accounts and join a vibrant, growing community of peers. 

Deep dive into 6 SAP TechEd tracks 

At SAP TechEd Virtual, you can choose from six content tracks covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions, each offering a variety of sessions and formats: 

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General 
  • Application Development and Automation 
  • Data and Analytics 
  • Integration 
  • Artificial Intelligence 

As always, the packed agenda includes keynotes, expert sessions with our Developer Advocates, lectures, and more. The full session catalog is now available.

SAP TechEd goes on tour 

To continue your learning journey beyond SAP TechEd Virtual, you can also participate in one of our in-person SAP TechEd on Tour stops and an additional event hosted by SAPinsider EMEA. 

Get excited for Devtoberfest 

Can’t get enough of SAP TechEd? Then don’t worry, we've got you covered. To extend the spirit of SAP TechEd even further and enable everyone in executing sought-after development skills all year round, Devtoberfest will become Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd and take place September 23 - October 4 and October 14 - 25. Block your calendars today to learn, code, collaborate, and earn the chance to win the grand prize! 

Register now and get an exclusive discount! 

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. As an SAP TechEd Virtual registrant, you can take advantage of an exciting package of curated learning, engagement, and solution experience offerings, including a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub. 

Register today for SAP TechEd Virtual, secure your discount and visit the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone, where you can acquire in-demand skills and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning to boost your career. 

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 1st, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/01/sap-developer-news-august-1st-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP Innobytes July edition

  • Video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CAVl-_HWHg8

August Developer Challenge

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-abap-core-data-services/ba-p/13778288

SAP TechEd Virtual Registration is Open!!

  • Registration Site: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code

  • Blog Post with Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/major-ux-update-of-joule-in-sap-build-code/ba-p/13770686

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP Innobytes July edition

2:09 August Developer Challenge

3:04 SAP TechEd Virtual Registration is Open!!

4:33 Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code

TRANSCRIPT

[DJ] The July edition of SAP BTP InnoBytes is out now. If you didn't know already, you should know SAP BTP InnoBytes is a monthly series of videos bringing you snackable, up-to-date information about innovations on SAP BTP. This month we see items on security and identity and also on the SAP Task Center. So first of all the SAP Secure Login Service for SAPGUI has some enhancements allowing you to connect to and use customer managed certificate authorities. There's support already for AWS private CAs with more options in the roadmap. With SAP Cloud Identity Services, there are some enhancements to allow you to define user group assignments at the application level on top of the already existing user profile and tenant balance. And finally, with the SAP Task Center there are some innovations allowing you now to bring in and use task providers from non-SAP and legacy systems, bringing a much asked for unified task view. Find out more about these innovations in the InnoBytes July edition. Link is in the description. And, by the way, it's the end of July, just starting August, which means that the Reverse APIs challenge is now coming to an end. You can still work through the tasks, but we've got an amazing challenge coming up for you for the month of August, and I'm going to hand over to my lovely friend and colleague Shilpa to tell you all about it. Shilpa, over to you.

[Shilpa] Thanks, DJ, in passing the SAP Developer Challenge. I must admit, you kept the SAP Community hungry in releasing eleven tasks in your challenge. Me and Sheena will try to keep up the legacy in making an interesting August Developer Challenge. So with that, hello, everyone. you might have seen the ongoing SAP TechByte series on ABAP Core Data Services. So with that, it's a good idea to have the developer challenge related to ABAP CDS. Watch out for every Wednesday where a new task will be released. There's a prerequisite which will help you to get started with this challenge. Now this blog post gives the information about the challenge, quick links, health document, and the timeline as and when the task is updated. Check out the blog post, make sure to complete the prerequisite, and be ready for the challenge.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. Have you heard? Registration for SAP TechEd Virtua 2024 is now open, and I'm not the only one that gets excited about this time of year. My best friend Sully also loves TechEd time, and he's gearing up as well. SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 will be packed with a ton of information for architects and developers, ranging from lectures and jumpstart sessions, from voice of the partner sessions to keynotes, and of course your very own developer keynote, which we are changing up a bit this year and having more of a deconstructed approach to the developer keynote. So stay tuned for more details on that. So make sure to sign up for SAP TechEd Virtual which takes place on October 8th and 9th and also stay tuned for more information around the SAP TechEd on tour stops starting in November. And lastly don't forget about Devtoberfest which starts two weeks prior to the start of SAP TechEd Virtual and picks up or picks back up for an additional two weeks after the SAP TechEd virtual event and as always your Developer Advocate team will be playing a huge role in both Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd in general and we are all really excited to see all of you soon bye for now

[Shrini] Hello SAP developers we are happy to announce some major UX announcements on Joule within SAP Build Code. The new design brings a more intuitive user interface which enhances usability and brings in streamlined workflows. Before the update, triggering Joule required navigating through specific entry points like the Guide Center or the Enhance button in the Data Editor. It also meant switching between separate chats for different tasks. With a new design, Joule has a single interface for all functionalities, making it easy to switch between tasks. There are some great new features to make all of this possible. Slash Commands lets you generate a Fiori limits application, get help on SAPUI5 topics, generate a CAP application, add sample data and logic to it. Context variables allows you to use hash symbol in combination with slash commands to reference existing project files. The dynamic suggestion feature shows developers' previous actions automatically. A new file management feature allows you to see which files Joule is using for code generation both during and after the process. A new file list block component allows developers to check the quality of the generated code in the files when multiple files are generated. Custom action buttons allows you to quickly navigate to various interfaces of base code and edit the generated results. If you want to dig deeper into these features, our design lead of SAP Build Code, Ivan, has written this blog post about it. We would love to hear your feedback on these topics.

entry.title: Choose your own adventure: The SAP TechEd Virtual session catalog is live!
entry.content:
I don’t know about you, but my excitement is building for the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual event – especially now that the session catalog is officially live! You know what that means, Tech friends – it’s time to check out all the offerings at sapteched.com and block your calendars for some serious, roll-up-your-sleeves learning.

Join us online October 8 – 9, 2024 and choose from over 100 sessions covering a wide range of topics to create your own agenda for a personalized SAP TechEd experience. Each day is filled with a number of different session types that cover topics from six unique tracks: SAP Business Technology Platform – General, Application Development and Automation, Data and Analytics, Integration and Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP. Bookmark the sessions that are valuable to you – and keep in mind that you can watch them both live and on-demand.

Start off TechEd Day 1 with a keynote in which our senior IT leaders and guests will reveal the latest news and announcements around top innovations and product developments. After the keynote, stick around to hear from SAP experts and partners who will share their own experiences and best-practices over the course of the two day event. Discover our different session types including Lectures, Track Intros, Jumpstarts, Keynote Deconstructed and Voice of Partner sessions.

There is so much to gain from SAP TechEd this year, I promise, you won’t want to miss it! Be sure to register for the SAP TechEd Virtual event by going here. And for those of you who want to continue your learning journey after SAP TechEd Virtual, please join us at one of the SAP TechEd on Tour stops or the SAP Insider EMEA 2024, a TechEd companion event. Learn more here.

Thanks, I hope to see everyone virtually or in person for SAP TechEd 2024!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 15th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/15/sap-developer-news-august-15th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd

  • Devtoberfest group: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest sessions: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events

Generative AI based Integration Flows and expose events via AIF

  • Generative AI based Integration Flow Generation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-integration-suite-generative-ai-based-integration-flow-generation/ba-p/13761166                                                                                                     
  • SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/a-natural-choice-sap-aif-can-now-expose-events-to-advanced-event-mesh-to/ba-p/13773758
  • SAP Note 3474406 - AIFAEM integration: https://me.sap.com/notes/3474406/E

CodeJam Release: CAP AI CodeJam - Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

  • CAP AI CodeJam GitHub repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm
  • Overview SAP CodeJams: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • RAG with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, GenAI Hub & CAP Live Stream: https://www.youtube.com/live/EkEbUYTfa6Q?si=LLS_EHRCW2AimvxO

ABAP Developer Challenge Week 2

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/task-2-cds-view-entity-associations-august-developer-challenge/m-p/13794132

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd

0:41 Generative AI based Integration Flows and expose events via AIF

1:29 CAP AI CodeJam - Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

2:04 ABAP Developer Challenge Week 2

Transcript

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. Devtoberfest is just around the corner, starting on September 23rd, and the Devtoberfest group in the SAP community is already live. Go there to read about the official contest and its rules, and also about the grand prize. Devtoberfest sessions are already being scheduled in the SAP Community as well, so go there to check them out, and hope to see you there. Bye!

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. In case you missed it, it is now possible to generate Integration Flows in Cloud Integration. You will just need to describe your integration scenario, and based on that description, you will create an Integration Flow. It is a new feature in Cloud Integration, which you will need to enable in the settings of your tenant. Check out Deepak's blog post for more information.

Also, you can now expose events to SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh by using the Application Interface Framework. What's cool about this is that you can even enable your on-premises ERP, for example, an ECC or an SAP S/4HANA. It is easy to create custom events by leveraging AIF. Check out Kosta's blog post for more details.

[Kevin] I'm really excited to announce a brand new CodeJam evolving around the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and the CAP LLM Plugin. This CodeJam will teach you how to build services on top of SAP AI Core and the SAP Generative AI Hub capabilities. So if you want to learn more about AI and how to build services on top of AI, follow the links in the description below to check out the CodeJam content on GitHub and check out all the additional information on our community platform. So see you in the next CodeJam.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope you're all having a great summer and I also hope that everyone is having a little fun with this month's Developer Challenge. Sheena and Shilpa kicked off your ABAP Developer Challenge last week with Task 1 and there was a huge response. Of course there was. It's ABAP and who doesn't love ABAP, right? I even found some time to complete Task 1 myself and I had a lot of fun doing it. This week's task was just released yesterday and already we have over 50 submissions out there. So I guess I better catch up and get going with Task 2 myself which is on the topic of CDS associations. So that's it for now. If you have a couple minutes for fun learning and a little challenge, check out the Developer Advocates ABAP Developer Challenge which is running now till the end of August. I'll put a link in the show notes. Bye for now!

entry.title: Build the Skills to Bring out Your Best: SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning Zone Is Now Live!
entry.content:
Calling all tech enthusiasts! With SAP TechEd Virtual being just around the corner, it’s time to get ahead of the game and build the skills to bring out your best. Join a community of experts, acquire in-demand skills, and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning in the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone – even before the main event starts on October 8!

Learning tools and opportunities for you:

  • Get started with 40 interactive value journeys and showcases to discover the value of SAP solutions.
  • Access 45 free, self-paced learning journeys curated to each of the content tracks of SAP TechEd Virtual to upskill in the latest SAP solutions. 
  • Subscribe to premium, guided learning resources with SAP Learning Hub to maximize your skills and earn a globally recognized certification, with an exclusive 25% discount.
  • Access demos and 25 basic trials to explore the power of SAP solutions and 50 use cases to get hands-on experience.
  • Connect with experts, expand your network, and collaborate with peers in dedicated learning groups and bridge the time until October 8 with SAP Community events.

Explore learning offerings for each SAP TechEd track:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Application Development and Automation
  • Data and Analytics
  • Integration

Register for SAP TechEd Virtual to benefit from a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub:

 As a registered SAP TechEd Virtual attendee, you can benefit from an exclusive 25% discount on your SAP Learning Hub subscription! Once you have subscribed to our guided premium learning offering, you can get access to learning content and certification exam attempts, expert guidance in digital live sessions and on-demand hands-on practice systems too.

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. Jump to the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone today to get a head start into SAP TechEd in 2024.

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone, and beyond!

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hey Everyone!

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅 October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 The ABAP sessions at this year's SAP TechEd event will be centered around "Clean Core Extensibility powered by ABAP Cloud and Generative AI". Attendees can look forward to a range of lectures (incl. roadmaps) and jump-starts on clean core development and transformation with ABAP Cloud and GenAI for all SAP S/4HANA editions, both cloud and on-premise. Below is an overview of the SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 sessions related to clean core extensibility and ABAP Cloud. 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024



Keynotes

KEY100CEST & KEY100EDT | SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
[Executive Keynote] At SAP TechEd, experts, developers, IT leaders, and our SAP Community gather to learn, share, and build together. Hear firsthand from SAP Board Members Juergen Mueller and Muhammad Alam how innovations in SAP Build solutions, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and other solutions across our end-to-end portfolio are revolutionizing the tech world.´
Speaker: 

  • Muhammad Alam, SAP Product Engineering, SAP
  • Michael Ameling, EVP and Chief Product Officer, SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP

Schedule: 
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 9:00 AM - 10:30 AM CEST [KEY100CEST]
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CEST [KEY100EDT]

DEV101A & DEV101B | Keynote recap + Community welcome
[Developer Keynote] Join the Developer Advocates as they welcome the SAP Community to SAP TechEd, provide a short recap of the key announcements, and share insights into what it means for developers. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 10:55 AM CEST [DEV101A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 5:30 PM - 5:55 PM CEST [DEV101B]

DEV100A  & DEV100B | Developer keynote [deconstructed]: Keeping the promise
[Developer Keynote Deconstructed] Hear from the developer advocates for a quick injection of news, recaps, and deep-dive demos covering various announcements from the event as well as other fun stuff. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST [DEV100A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST [DEV100B]

Track Overviews 

AD100 – Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions
[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, discover how to seamlessly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth. 
Speakers: Dr Alexander Rother (SAP), Thomas Volmering (SAP), Björn Schulz (REWE digital GmbH)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

DT100 – Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
[Track Overview, 45 min] Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.
Speakers: Jan Gilg (SAP), Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 11:15 AM CEST

Lectures

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build 
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions. 
Speakers: Dr. Anne Keller (SAP), Tim Back (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

AD107 – Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer capabilities
[Lecture, 25 min] Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks. 
Speakers: Dr. Jasmin Gruschke (SAP), Sebastian Baskovich (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  9:00 PM - 9:30 PM CEST

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

AD106 – Clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA with the latest ABAP Cloud features
[Lecture, 25 min] ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions on both SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP S/4HANA, helping ensure clean core compliance by definition. Learn about the latest features offered in ABAP Cloud and how generative AI can boost the development efficiency. 
Speaker: Volker Drees (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

DT201 – Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] The extensibility portfolio for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition consist both on-stack and side-by-side extension techniques. In this lecture you will learn how on-stack key user extensibility can be used to develop a custom business object together with an application in SAP Build Apps. 
Speaker: Dr. Thomas Schneider (SAP), Alda Dollani (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 2:30 PM - 2:55 PM CEST

DT200 – Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.
Speaker:  Olga Dolinskaja (SAP), Thomas Fiedler (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:00 PM - 3:25 PM CEST

AI104 – Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot
[Lecture, 25 min] Developer capabilities in the ABAP programming language in the Joule copilot can boost productivity and simplify the extension of SAP applications. Explore real-world use cases, upcoming releases, and ways Joule capabilities – such as generating ABAP business objects, completing and explaining code, and creating unit tests – can transform your development process. See how Joule can enhance everyday work and support clean core implementations.
Speaker: Salma Sohrabi-Jahromi (SAP), Jens Weiler (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:30 PM - 03:55 PM CEST

Jump-Starts

AD180 – Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud powered by Joule's ABAP Developer capabilities
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model supported by generative AI. You will also learn about features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only treeviews for displaying hierarchical data. 
ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building clean core compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on-premise. 
Speaker: Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

DT280 – Building Clean Core Extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to speed up the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensions with ABAP Cloud using two new ABAP Development Tools (ADT) based generators for adding extension fields and for building custom UI services on top of business objects that have been released by SAP. 
Speaker: Andre Fischer (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out this overview of the ABAP sessions at Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024 - which will take place virtually from Sept 23 to Oct 25, 2024 - and watch the replays of last year's events, SAP TechEd Virtual in 2023 and SAP's Devtoberfest in 2023.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 22nd, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/22/sap-developer-news-august-22nd-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP CodeJams LATAM Wrap Up

  • Request your own SAP CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415

ABAP CDS 2408 Release & External Entities

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/174427
  • SAP Help Portal  Link - https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-data-models/cds-external-entities?version=s4hana_cloud

SAP TechEd Session Catalog is Live!

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

SAP Open Source Licenses and AI Webinar

  • Registration links: internal / external participants

sap.ui.mdc library available for productive usage

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-and-flexible-sap-ui-mdc-released-for-productive-use/ba-p/13793439
  • Samples: https://ui5.sap.com/#/entity/sap.ui.mdc/sample/sap.ui.mdc.demokit.sample.TableFilterBarJson
  • Tutorial: https://github.com/SAP-samples/ui5-mdc-json-tutorial

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP CodeJams LATAM Wrap Up

1:19 ABAP CDS Release News 2408 – External Entities

2:58 TechEd Session Catalog is Live

3:37 SAP Open Source Licenses and AI Webinar

4:33 sap.ui.mdc library available for productive usage

Transcript

[Antonio and Josh] Hola, SAP developers. Hey, everybody. It's Josh. And Antonio. We are so pleased with the attendance, participation, and the feedback from these eight, ocho, CodeJams we just finished yesterday. Exactly. So from Sao Paulo, Santiago, Buenos Aires, and Bolotá, we experienced four great days focused on event-driven integrations and another four great days focused on SAP Build Apps, and a few Stammtisch as well. Besides the amazing technology and the people, the locations, I also learned something new. We traveled all over another continent. My first time here in South America, Latown, but I felt so at home. We talked SAP, shared community stories, and we chatted about Devtoberfest and TechEd. Agreed. I was quite happy to get to meet many developers around here and colleagues in this region as well. So I'm looking forward to meeting more in the near future. Yep. So that's it for this road trip. Thank you all so much. Muchas gracias. See you next time. Y lazamos, muchos gracias. Ciao.

[Sheena] The standard database of application server ABAP is SAP HANA database. And SAP ABAP program requires access to another database we use to configure a secondary connection. Andrea Schlotter, in her latest blog post on the 24.08 release of ABAP CDS, explains a new and exciting feature, CDS external entities. CDS external entities help to retrieve data from multiple other remote sources, which can be SAP HANA or non-SAP HANA databases. Unlike traditional secondary connections that limit access to a single database and require exact matching of remote and local objects, CDS external entities offer a more flexible and efficient approach by allowing access to multiple remote data sources within a single SQL statement. This feature leverages the data federation approach of the SAP HANA Smart Data Access technology. The blog post also talks about the comparison of secondary connection and external entities, how to access data in ABAP SQL, and establish outbound SQL access using an external entity. Please note that currently, only dynamic external entities are supported, which can only be accessed using ABAP SQL and cannot be used as a data source for other CDS entities. Also note that the dynamic external entities allow only read access, and writable external entities are planned for one of the upcoming releases. For details, check out the links in the description.

[Tom] Planning for TechEd Virtual 2024 is officially in full swing. And I'm thrilled to report that the session catalog is now live. Now is the time to explore all of the learning opportunities we have in store during our free virtual event this October. You'll find over 90 live sessions, including live lectures, code demos, track overviews, jumpstart tutorials, and expert analysis. Signing up is simple, just visit sapteched.com and register for our free virtual event on October 8th and 9th. So sign up now and I'll see you online in October.

[Ajay] Hello, SAP developers, greetings of the day. Generative AI, LLMs, tools such as Copilot and Codestral, everybody is in it now. But what does it mean in terms of responsibilities in the daily work of a developer in an enterprise context? SAP Open Source Webinar on Topic, Open Source Licenses within the context of AI is available on September 3rd, 2024 at 3 p.m. CEST. Georgi, who is an open source champion at SAP, will give an overview of illegal environment surrounding AI, talk about restrictions and obligations of licenses used for AI components, and give you examples of technical and regulatory solutions to avoid IP infringements. If this sounds interesting to you, do not miss this opportunity. Links to the registrations are in the description below.

[Nico] Hi, everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The SAP UI MDC library is now available for productive usage, and this is since SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 version 1.124. The MDC controls, which is short for metadata-driven controls, by the way, help you build smart controls and even whole applications following the SAP Fiori design guidelines. The SAP Fiori Elements flexible programming model for RoadAW4 also use these controls under the hood. But they work with other protocols such as plain REST as well. Check out these samples in the UI5 documentation, as well as the corresponding tutorial on GitHub. Links are in the description, and I hope to see you soon.

entry.title: Real Talk with SAP TechEd Track Leads
entry.content:
Ready to get to know the experts who will be shaping your SAP TechEd experience this year? Introducing Real Talk with SAP TechEd Track Leads, where you'll meet the minds behind the tracks in this 6-part personal blog series leading up to the October virtual event days.

Every #TrackLeadTuesday, we'll reveal a fresh new intro blog from one or two of our 2024 track leads, bringing you closer to the SAP insiders guiding your upcoming TechEd journey. Learn more about what drives their passion, what's in store from their focus topic, and more.

On the Roster

  • Meet Claire | Application Development & Automation Track Lead
  • Meet Konstantin & Stephanie | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads
  • Meet Katya & Jerome | Artificial Intelligence Track Leads
  • Meet Eduardo & Britta | Data & Analytics Track Leads
  • Meet Suna | Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP Track Lead
  • Meet Shilpa & Ananth | Integration Track Leads

Ask, Share, Connect

Networking with fellow attendees and experts remains one of the top benefits for participating in SAP TechEd. But why wait? Let's start the conversation now! Our track leads can't wait to meet you. Here are some suggestions to help give them a warm welcome:

  • Introduce Yourself: Comment your name, job, and a little about yourself and what you're excited for at TechEd.
  • Share Your TechEd Toolkit: An open mind? Good notepad? Share what tools are must-haves for you at TechEd and see what others are packing.
  • Ask a Question: Something track-specific you’re curious about? Ask our track leads in the comments.

See you in the comments!

entry.title: Real Talk with Claire | Application Development & Automation Track Lead
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members 👋 I am Ching Wei (Claire) Tseng, a Product Manager at SAP Build. Originally from Taiwan, I studied at Mannheim University in Germany, earning my master’s degree in 2018 before joining SAP. In my free time, I enjoy cooking Taiwanese, Japanese, and Italian dishes. I’m also an avid traveler and museum enthusiast. 

In the Application Development and Automation (ADA) track of TechEd 2024, you can harness the power of SAP Build—a suite of low-code and pro-code solutions powered by generative AI—to accelerate application development, automation, and business site creation. Explore the latest innovations in ABAP, Build Code, and SAP Build for low-code to extend your ERP system while keeping the core clean!

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

We cover a wide range of topics, including SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, ABAP, SAPUI5, SAP Build Code, Enterprise Automation with SAP, SAP Mobile Services and more. I’m excited to collaborate with so many outstanding speakers and product managers. Stay tuned, as we will be announcing numerous new features and great news at TechEd!

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit?

Our exceptional speakers will showcase various practical demos during the lectures, allowing you to learn how to apply our products and solutions in real business scenarios and enhance enterprise performance.

In AD181 jump-start session, you can gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. In another jump-start session AD180, you’ll learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in the ABAP Cloud model using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model, enhanced by generative AI.

Track stars (sessions to keep on your radar)

  1. AD110 Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build: Experience the power of Joule copilot in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process.
  2. AD104 SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP: Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how Joule copilot can speed up your extension development.
  3.  AD105 Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build: Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.
  4. AD109 Experience the next level of Enterprise Automation with SAP: Explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together.
  5. AD103 Extend your SAP solutions with custom mobile apps using SAP Build Code: Learn to transform your user experience with SAP Mobile Services and AI capabilities in SAP Build Code.
  6. AD107 Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer capabilities: Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks. 

Most important of all, you can’t miss out on our intro session: AD100 Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions. Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean and learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development!

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Laptop, Pad or phone 📱: As TechEd is 100% virtual this year, you can join TechEd easily any place!
  • Curiosity and an Open Mind 💡: TechEd is a great opportunity to learn and explore new technologies, come ready to engage with SAP experts and absorb as much as you can!
  • Agenda 📆: Pre-plan your interested sessions and keynotes to maximize your time.

Don't miss out! Explore the TechEd 2024 session catalog and ensure your favorite ADA track sessions are on your list. We're excited to connect with you at the upcoming TechEd sessions, where you can tap into the full potential of SAP BTP with both pro-code and low-code extensibility options.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 29th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/29/sap-developer-news-august-29th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

  • Release Notes Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

SAP TechEd Registration

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

  • New CodeJam in the topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • CodeJam event in Warsaw, Poland: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ec-p/13809704#M572

ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

  • ABAP Developer Challenge: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-abap-core-data-services/ba-p/13778288
  • Devtoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

2:15 SAP TechEd Registration

3:08 New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

4:04 ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

Transcript

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here, and yes it is time for yet again another release of the BTP ABAP environment, release 2408. And once again, this release is jam packed with new features for ABAP developers. Just to name a few, ABAP Developer Tools now supports a read-only fallback editor for development objects that do not have a native editor, for example BSPs, and also ADT now supports displaying warning messages coming from the HANA database while debugging nested AMDPs. CDS now supports external databases via ABAP CDS and ABAP SQL using CDS external entities. We also have support for logical external schemas for outbound SQL access with external entities. There has been updates to the ABAP repository object wizard, which now includes generating ABAP CDS provider models. There is also new support for creating CDS aspects to store and reuse field definitions and calculations. and we also have support for a few new annotations as well.

There are a bunch of new features around UI services in Fiori Launchpad, like support for adding a custom logo to the Launchpad and other customizing of the browser window. A lot of new features around integration topics like direct push-based integration over RFC, new enhancements around the advanced event mesh, and creating and assigning API packages.

Now I've only touched on a few of the new features to hit release 24.08. So, if you want the full story and all of the details, be sure to check out the 24.08 release blog post today. See you next time.

[Josh] It's time for SAP TechEd. Every year, TechEd is our time to get together, share ideas, learn, and have a lot of fun. Last year's TechEd was awesome, and good news, registration for TechEd 2024 is officially open. We've got an incredible TechEd lineup. First off, two full days of keynote analysis, code demos over 80 live sessions, three channels of programming, and live Q&A. That's right, they're still letting me talk live on a microphone. Then, and I am so excited about this, join us on tour as we make our way around the world with in-person stops on three continents throughout 2024 and 2025. We can't wait to connect in person for deep dive sessions, hands-on labs, and continue learning together. And trust me, this is a TechEd you will not wanna miss. See you online in October.

[Nora] Hey there, we have a new Generative AI CodeJam on the list. The first two events are already scheduled. One of them will be on the 20th of September in Warsaw, Poland at the KMD office and the other one will be right after Virtual TechEd in Munich. During the CodeJam you will learn how to access different large range models via Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. We will use the Python SDK of Generative AI Hub and we will also use the SAP AI Launchpad to access the models. You will also learn how to implement a retrieval augmented generation use case using the HANA Vector Engine and we will have a look at different chunking techniques, how you can chunk your documents right and then we will also have a look at a multi modal model where you will learn how to work with images and text. So, make sure to also check out all the other great code gems on the list, find an event near you or host your own event. See you there! Hello, SAP developers.

[Shilpa] I hope you are enjoying the August Developer Challenge on ABAP Core Data Services. The last task in this four-week challenge was out yesterday. We have seen huge response over 550 plus submissions throughout the challenge and thanks to each one of you for taking time and as well as involving in some good discussions on best practices. For those of you who have not yet taken part in the challenge, you have one more week left until we close it. In this four-week task, you will learn about the basics of ABAP series, annotations, associations, criticality and object page. While this challenge is coming to an end, we have another exciting event kicking off in September and that is Devtoberfest. We are back again with lots of interesting sessions which ranges from September 23rd until October 25th. Like last year, we have dedicated topics each day ranging from ABAP, CAP, SAP Build, Build Code, integration, machine learning, AI, and front-end. Most of the sessions are already scheduled in the community event page. Check it out and happy learning!

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Kunal Bansal 🏅: How SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 Can Help Boost Your Career
entry.content:
When @KunalBansal booked his hotel in Bangalore near the 2023 SAP TechEd venue, he knew he wouldn’t be spending much time there. Instead, his real focus was on the learning, networking, and upskilling at SAP’s premier technology event - TechEd. “Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says, reflecting on his experience. Attending SAP TechEd in-person for the 2nd time, he discovered just how powerful in-person networking and knowledge-sharing could be. “It’s the best place to connect, ask questions, and exchange ideas,” Kunal emphasizes. 

His enthusiasm shines through as he recalls the inspiring keynote delivered by SAP Board Members, which focused on the transformative potential of GenAI. He also cherishes the time spent at the SAP Community booth, engaging with fellow SAP Mentors and Champions. “I’m definitely attending again this year,” he says with a smile. 

With SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 on October 8-9 just weeks away, Kunal is busy curating his agenda. He’s carefully selecting sessions that will provide the most value, both professionally and personally. 

Kunal's Top Picks for SAP TechEd Virtual in 2024:

  • XP101 | SAP BTP strategy and highlights  
    Get firsthand insights into SAP’s BTP strategy and gain a deeper understanding of how SAP BTP unifies development, automation, data, and AI into one powerful platform, driving digital transformation. 
  • IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 
    Discover how to accelerate innovation by integrating business systems using SAP Integration Suite, enabling real-time, scalable enterprise-wide integration.  
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
    Learn how to transition smoothly from legacy systems to the SAP cloud, leveraging SAP Integration Suite’s robust tools, best practices and services for a seamless migration. 

Make sure you also check out Kunal's entire expert session playlist for SAP TechEd Virtual!

SAP Learning site is his go-to resource

“Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says.During the week, Kunal is fully immersed in his role as an Advisory Architect at IBM, leveraging over 13 years of experience in SAP CX Suites. His deep expertise in SAP Intelligent Enterprise and SAP Solutions underscores his commitment to staying current in the field.

“I work closely with stakeholders to deliver scalable, compliant, and high-performing solutions using SAP technologies,” Kunal explains. His role demands a unique blend of technical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong communication skills to drive successful project outcomes. “And to stay current, I dedicate at least an hour daily to learning.” 

Kunal often turns to the SAP Learning site to stay updated with the rapidly evolving technologies. “The free SAP Learning portal has been my go-to resource for learning journeys, leading to SAP Certification, while Learning Hub and Partner Edge portals have deepened my concepts to deliver greater business value for clients". 

Strengthened credibility, solidified reputation

While dedicating an hour a day to learning can seem daunting at first, Kunal says it’s worth it, as it has become a crucial part of his journey, pushing him to achieve greater heights and inspiring others. “Earning SAP Certifications through continuous learning has been a game-changer for my career growth, unlocking opportunities for more complex, high-impact projects”. On a more personal level, Kunal notes, “SAP Certifications have strengthened my credibility and solidified my reputation as a thought leader in the SAP Community.” 

As Kunal finalizes his SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 agenda, he’s also exploring the recently launched SAP TechEd Virtual Learning Zone to discover further learning tools and opportunities mapped to the six SAP TechEd Virtual content tracks. “I’m excited to dive into Developing with SAP Integration Suite to keep myself engaged until SAP TechEd Virtual kicks off,” Kunal says, eagerly anticipating the innovations in SAP Business AI and SAP BTP. 

Now it's your time to shine!

What sessions are you most excited about, and what’s your favorite resource from the SAP TechEd Virtual - Learning Zone? Share your thoughts in the comments and make sure to visit the SAP TechEd group on SAP Community to connect with peers!

entry.title: Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads
entry.content:
We are Konstantin Heine and Stephanie Stang, Product Managers for SAP BTP. We look forward to the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual – especially towards the SAP BTP General track.

Konstantin: As a child, I always played with interlocking building blocks. This led to my career, which now has to do with a modular platform. But jokes aside, I find it incredibly fascinating to see the multifaceted ways SAP BTP is utilized. In my role, I focus on making the development and operation of applications on SAP BTP easier, while also providing supporting assets to navigate certain challenges.

Stephanie:  While taking care of the SAP BTP cross roadmap topics and creating awareness for our customers, I discover every day what you can get out of SAP BTP. But even in private, I like to discover a lot. That’s why I like travelling around the world and getting to experience new adventures.

The SAP Business Technology Platform track is designed to provide an overview of the key technologies that make up the platform. We cover a broad range of topics and grouped them into three sub-tracks that help you to easily find sessions of interest:

  1. Strategy & Overview – Starting point to learn everything about SAP BTP and strategic topics.
  2. Guidance & Operations – Content on how to get started or advanced content on guidance and best practices.
  3. Security & Compliance – Contains sessions that help you understand how to securely operate and integrate your applications.

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

Our track provides insights into future trends with guidance on architecture, methodologies, and governance models to help define your path forward. Every SAP BTP application requires guidance for architecture, operations, and security. Regular discussions on these topics with customers and partners further enhance and improve our content.

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

In our SAP BTP General track, you’ll discover technology innovations that bring ideas to life. Sessions offer an overview of the platform’s key technologies, getting started guidance, best practices, and how to operate and securely integrate your applications. Begin with hands-on experience in the SAP Discovery Center or explore reference architectures within the SAP BTP Guidance Framework, covering your SAP BTP journey end-to-end.

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar)

  1. XP101 - SAP BTP strategy and highlights: See how SAP BTP brings together all pillars and get insights into our development priorities, explore upcoming innovation highlights, and come away with a clear picture of the platform’s future direction.
  2. XP105 - Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption: Learn how to get started with SAP BTP and what assets exist to design, develop, and operate enterprise-grade applications on SAP BTP.
  3. XP103 - Got security? Services for security and compliance with SAP BTP: Explore the services SAP BTP offers around security as well as identity and access management. 
  4. XP201 - Observability of custom-built CAP applications with SAP Cloud ALM: Learn how you can instrument your custom applications based on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), using OpenTelemetry.
  5. XP180 - Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center: This Jump-Start session allows you to explore the latest enhancements in speed of project implementation and personalization in SAP Discovery Center. 

And don’t miss the Track Overview session with both of us: XP100 - Unlock business innovation and achieve long-term success with SAP BTP.  Together, we will deep-dive into the sessions and discuss some of the highlights presented throughout the track.

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Coffee: Could be quite useful managing to watch the broad range of TechEd sessions.
  • Agenda Builder: Allows you to build your individual agenda and avoid colliding sessions (this will probably happen, but don’t worry as you can watch the recordings later).
  • Excitement and a lot of questions: Make sure to use the Q&A tool during sessions. We will be answering questions live in the session and in the tool.

Excited for what you will gain out of our track? Then join us at SAP TechEd Virtual in October and register today.
